channel.title: Orthodox Christian Sermons: 5th Sunday of Pascha:The Samaritan Woman
channel.description: Audio Orthodox Christian Sermons: 5th Sunday of Pascha:The Samaritan Woman

item.title: 5th Sunday of Pascha, The Samaritan Woman, She left her waterpot., 2008-05-25

item.title: 5th Sunday of Pascha, The Samaritan Woman, A textbook lesson in how to acquire the Holy Spirit, 2009-05-17
item.description: :5th Sunday of Pascha. The Samaritan Woman, A textbook lesson in how to acquire the Holy Spirit, 2009-05-17
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :5th Sunday of Pascha. The Samaritan Woman, A textbook lesson in how to acquire the Holy Spirit, 2009-05-17

item.title: 5th Sunday of Pascha, The Samaritan Woman, 2010-05-02
item.description: :5th Sunday of Pascha. The story of the woman at the well, the Samaritan woman, Photini, must be understood in a historical, theological, mystical and practical context. She said "the well is deep", and this story can be discussed from many perspectives, many of which are, alas, unintelligible to most because of their low level of theological knowledge and interest. The most important one for us is what we must do do get the living water, the Holy Spirit. Saint Photini shows us several things that we MUST be if we are to be saved - theologically literate, having thirst for righteousness, humility, and zeal. Her conversation with the Lord and subsequent actions show all these things.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :5th Sunday of Pascha. The story of the woman at the well, the Samaritan woman, Photini, must be understood in a historical, theological, mystical and practical context. She said "the well is deep", and this story can be discussed from many perspectives, many of which are, alas, unintelligible to most because of their low level of theological knowledge and interest. The most important one for us is what we must do do get the living water, the Holy Spirit. Saint Photini shows us several things that we MUST be if we are to be saved - theologically literate, having thirst for righteousness, humility, and zeal. Her conversation with the Lord and subsequent actions show all these things.

item.title: 5th Sunday of Pascha, The Samaritan Woman, A textbook example of how to listen to God. , 2011-05-22
item.description: :5th Sunday of Pascha. The Samaritan Woman, A textbook example of how to listen to God. , 2011-05-22
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :5th Sunday of Pascha. The Samaritan Woman, A textbook example of how to listen to God. , 2011-05-22

item.title: 5th Sunday of Pascha, The Samaritan Woman, 2012-05-13
item.description: :5th Sunday of Pascha. The story of the woman at the well, St Photini (Svetlana) has an enormous amount of deep and important theology, but the most important part is the personality of the Samaritan woman must be understood an emulated to understand any of it. She was very humble; when the Lord exposed her sin, she stayede with Him to hear more. When she understood Him, she left her waterpot. We esplore these two actions. She was an extremist. We cannot be saved unless we are too.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :5th Sunday of Pascha. The story of the woman at the well, St Photini (Svetlana) has an enormous amount of deep and important theology, but the most important part is the personality of the Samaritan woman must be understood an emulated to understand any of it. She was very humble; when the Lord exposed her sin, she stayede with Him to hear more. When she understood Him, she left her waterpot. We esplore these two actions. She was an extremist. We cannot be saved unless we are too.

item.title: 5th Sunday of Pascha, 2013-06-03
item.description: :5th Sunday of Pascha. The conversation and conversion of the Samaritan woman by the well, recorded only in John, is the conversation of Christ with the soul. This is good news for us, as the future Equal to the Apostles Photini (Svetlana) was not a good woman - she had many sins - and we are not good either, however, she listened to the Lord, even as He sternly corrected her false beliefs and immoral lifestyle. The same conversation is happening to us, every day. Are you listening? Do you know how to listen? St Photini teaches us.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :5th Sunday of Pascha. The conversation and conversion of the Samaritan woman by the well, recorded only in John, is the conversation of Christ with the soul. This is good news for us, as the future Equal to the Apostles Photini (Svetlana) was not a good woman - she had many sins - and we are not good either, however, she listened to the Lord, even as He sternly corrected her false beliefs and immoral lifestyle. The same conversation is happening to us, every day. Are you listening? Do you know how to listen? St Photini teaches us.

item.title: 5th Sunday of Pascha, Sunday of the Samaritan Woman, 2014-05-18
item.description: :5th Sunday of Pascha. The personality of the Great "Equal to the Apostles&qot;, Photini (Svetlana) shines in the story of her encounter with Jesus at the well. We can learn many things from her. We must learn to have her honest and courage, her inquiring heart, and cultivate within us the burning love she had for Christ. Her life also teaches us that we can recover and be healed from ANYTHING. Observe her path to full healing, and walk it!
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :5th Sunday of Pascha. The personality of the Great "Equal to the Apostles&qot;, Photini (Svetlana) shines in the story of her encounter with Jesus at the well. We can learn many things from her. We must learn to have her honest and courage, her inquiring heart, and cultivate within us the burning love she had for Christ. Her life also teaches us that we can recover and be healed from ANYTHING. Observe her path to full healing, and walk it!
